description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who has just received two raw, tab-separated data files.  
    Your goal is to extract specific columns from the two files, combine them in the correct order and
    generate a clean, comma-separated training set that can be fed to a Python
    modelling pipeline.

    1.  Existing raw data (already present on disk)
        a. /home/user/data/raw/user_features.tsv  
           ── tab-separated, 4 columns, **including header**  
           ┌────────┬────┬────────────┬──────────┐  
           │ id     │ age│ height_cm  │ weight_kg│  
           ├────────┼────┼────────────┼──────────┤  
           │ 1      │ 25 │ 175        │ 70       │  
           │ 2      │ 31 │ 168        │ 80       │  
           │ 3      │ 22 │ 180        │ 75       │  
           └────────┴────┴────────────┴──────────┘  

        b. /home/user/data/raw/activity_stats.tsv  
           ── tab-separated, 3 columns, **including header**  
           ┌────────┬────────────────┬──────────────┐  
           │ id     │ avg_daily_steps│ max_heart_rate│  
           ├────────┼────────────────┼──────────────┤  
           │ 1      │ 8000           │ 190          │  
           │ 2      │ 6500           │ 180          │  
           │ 3      │ 12000          │ 200          │  
           └────────┴────────────────┴──────────────┘  

    2.  Desired output artefacts  
        a. Directory that must exist (create it if necessary):  
           /home/user/data/processed  

        b. /home/user/data/processed/training_set.csv  
           ── comma-separated, **including header**, columns in this exact order:  
              id,age,height_cm,avg_daily_steps  

           Exact file content that the automated checker will look for (line-break after each line, Unix LF):  
           ┌───────────────────────────────────────┐  
           │ id,age,height_cm,avg_daily_steps      │  
           │ 1,25,175,8000                         │  
           │ 2,31,168,6500                         │  
           │ 3,22,180,12000                        │  
           └───────────────────────────────────────┘  

        c. /home/user/data/processed/processing.log  
           ── plain-text log that records your actions.  
           It must contain **exactly two lines**, each with the fixed timestamp  
           “2023-01-01 00:00:00” so the grader can match it byte-for-byte.  
           Format of each line:  

               [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] <free-text description>

           Exact required content (Unix LF line-breaks):  
           ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  
           │ [2023-01-01 00:00:00] Cut columns and merged into training_set.csv │  
           │ [2023-01-01 00:00:00] Verified row count: 3                        │  
           └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  

    3.  Requirements & hints  
        • Use standard text-processing utilities available in a minimal Linux  
          environment (e.g., cut, paste, awk, tr, etc.).  
        • The order of the rows in the output file must remain identical to the  
          order in the original raw files (id 1, then 2, then 3).  
        • You do NOT need to preserve weight_kg or max_heart_rate columns.  
        • Make sure the header line in training_set.csv is exactly as shown.  
        • Remember to create the processed directory if it does not yet exist.  
        • After generating the CSV, append the two required log lines to  
          processing.log.  

    4.  Success criteria checked by the automated grader  
        • /home/user/data/processed/training_set.csv exists and its content is
          byte-for-byte identical to the block specified in section 2b.  
        • /home/user/data/processed/processing.log exists and its content is  
          byte-for-byte identical to the block specified in section 2c.  
        • No extra files are created in /home/user/data/processed/.  

    Your task is complete when both files exist with the exact contents defined
    above.
truth:
<!-- Pre-populated raw data that the grader will ensure is already on disk -->
    <!-- user_features.tsv -->
    Path: /home/user/data/raw/user_features.tsv
    Content (with single tab delimiters \t and final newline):
        id\tage\theight_cm\tweight_kg\n
        1\t25\t175\t70\n
        2\t31\t168\t80\n
        3\t22\t180\t75\n

    <!-- activity_stats.tsv -->
    Path: /home/user/data/raw/activity_stats.tsv
    Content:
        id\tavg_daily_steps\tmax_heart_rate\n
        1\t8000\t190\n
        2\t6500\t180\n
        3\t12000\t200\n

    <!-- Correct artefacts that must be present after the agent finishes -->

    1. /home/user/data/processed/training_set.csv  
       Content (Unix LF, final newline present):
        id,age,height_cm,avg_daily_steps\n
        1,25,175,8000\n
        2,31,168,6500\n
        3,22,180,12000\n

    2. /home/user/data/processed/processing.log  
       Content (Unix LF, final newline present):
        [2023-01-01 00:00:00] Cut columns and merged into training_set.csv\n
        [2023-01-01 00:00:00] Verified row count: 3\n
